summary: Script is excellent: proper type hints, error handling, clean architecture (parse→aggregate→render). Workflow follows GitHub Actions best practices: pinned actions (v4, v5), least-privilege permissions (contents: read), correct job dependencies (needs:), proper bash error handling (set -euo pipefail), and appropriate variable quoting. Minor issues: (1) fixture directory fallback logic (lines 27-32) is over-engineered—checks fixtures-input then falls back to tests/fixtures with no comment explaining intent or why flexibility is needed; (2) glob pattern handling is correct but could be clearer; (3) aggregator silently processes 0 results before checking if files match (mitigated by later error on empty list). Script itself is maintainable and extensible. Would ship the script as-is; simplify or document the fixture strategy in the workflow.
